seoTitle: Ooma vs Ophir — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Ooma and Ophir. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Ooma and Ophir.
heading: Ooma vs Ophir — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Ooma, New South Wales and Ophir, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Ooma has a lower overall climate risk score (33/100) compared to Ophir (39/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Ooma or Ophir safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Ooma has a flood risk score of 35/100, while Ophir scores 42/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Ooma scores 33/100 for bushfire risk. Ophir scores 64/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Ooma or Ophir?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Ooma has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.33% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
